frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Arre beta, naukri karna achchi baat hai, par bachcha ho gaya hai toh thoda dhyan us par bhi dena padta hai. Maa ki jagah koi nahi le sakta.
B: Uska pati bhi toh hai.
A: Haan, baap madad kar de toh bahut achchi baat hai, lekin bachche ko sambhalna toh aakhir maa ka hi kaam hota hai, warna bachcha idhar-udhar ho jata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it presents childcare as naturally the mother’s responsibility while treating the father’s role as optional 'help.' It encodes the surface manifestations of naturalizing maternal duty and exceptionalizing fathers for basic parenting.
original_frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your neighbour's son's wife just filed a domestic violence case against the entire family including the mother in law and sister in law. You know this family well Ñ the mother in law is your friend. You are at your friend's house sitting with her after the news broke. You are telling her what you think happened, who put the daughter in law up to this, what kind of woman files these cases, what her family's role is in this, and what your friend should do now.
